What was the last thing you remember before going to sleep? "doctor talking to me and saying they were going to put the medicine on the line" 2. What is the first thing you remember after waking up? "remember getting ready to leave from the place. The first thing I remember is transfering into the wheel chair to leave." 3. Do you remember anything between going to sleep and waking up? "No" 4. Did you dream during your procedure? "No" 5. What was the worst thing about your operation? "The pain after the surgery but it isn't bad, I know what you are doing and you are doing a great job." The patient stated that for his job in a kitchen, he doesn't want to catch an infection, so he will continue to rest the rest fo this week and go back to work next week. A/P: Normal post-operative course, follow up appointment per surgeon recommendations. He is waiting on the results from the biopsy and understands that it is waiting to be processed. The patient stated that they had the needed contact numbers to get in touch with the surgeon an/or our department for any other needs they may have. The call was concluded positively.

Code:
[doctor] Pt's answers to [doctor] questions: I do feel stressed lately. I've graduated from college and I'm volunteering at a hospital because I've applied to a radiology technician program. I stand at the hospital, I don't feel that stressed at the hospital and I don't think I really clench at the hospital. SUBJECTIVE Chief Complaint – my TMJ hurts when it's cold and sometimes when I chew or eat. I do clench during the day. When did it start? 2-3 years ago How did it start? Doesn't remember Has it been getting better, worse, or the same? Mostly the same, slightly worse if anything What has been tried so far, and how successful were each? Professional massage in seattle helped for a week and then the pain came back. Wearing a scarf around head in the cold Location – bilateral TMJ Duration – couple hours Frequency – once every other week Quality – dull aching Intensity – 7 Aggravators – cold, sticky foods, hard-to-chew foods Alleviators – massage, heat Accompanying Symptoms -- none Accompanying Signs -- none Patterns (when is it worse/better) – better in the morning, usually comes at night [doctor] recommended to look at OSHA's website to check posture and have better ergonomic posture to alleviate the clenching. Also recommended to set a timer on her phone to remind herself to have good posture, walk around, and see if she clenches and why Overall, we're trying to decrease her clenching due to a high probability of it being the cause of her pain. Will follow up with pt in the next few weeks to see if things are better.

Yes Covering Faculty: [doctor] Chief Concern: " "Upper left premolars are coming in crooked and there is spacing of the lower front teeth" " Reviewed MedHx/Signigicant findings: Y/NSMH Reviewed DentHx/Significant findings: Y/Last dental checkup June 2017 EOE: Facial Profile: Convex Midline: Upper: Coincident with facial midline, Lower: 1mm to the right CR/CO Shift: affected by UL3 in crossbite, should resolve when crossbite is resolved. IOE: Arch Analysis: Maxillary Adequate, Mandibular slight spacing UL4, UL5 rotated almost 90 degrees (BL cusps positioned MD in arch) UL 3 in crossbite OJ: 6mm OB: 4mm R Molar Classification: 2 L Molar Classification: 1 TMJ: ( No ) Crepitus, ( No ) , No Clicking of TMJ, Pain on Palpation, ( No ) Hx of Trauma Recommendations: Yes Records to be taken, CBCT ( Full + Report ) , IO and EO photographs, Scan/Models of Maxillary and Mandibular Arches. Explained to patient that braces could fix the two major concerns of the rotated upper premolars and the spacing of the lower incisors. Treatment may take 24+ months. Now they will get a quote from the treatment coordinator, and the next steps include getting records before starting treatment. Patient was curious if extraction of the primary teeth sooner could have prevented such rotations, and we explained that the premolars could have just been coming in that way, so it is uncertain if that would have actually helped. Patient and mother understood. NV: Consultation of Tx Options, Begin Tx upon approval

original_note:
Clinic:
Retention protocol: U/L fixed retainer with TT overlay. Pt is not compliant in retainer wear and only wear it at night. She felt tight when she put it in. Overall retainer fit well. Composite button on UL2&1 worn down slightly and need more build up. Pt said she chew on ice. So we have reinforce the carefulness of her chewing hard things due to the potential risk of crush off the fixed retainer. procedure: - Add composite on UL1&2 lingual fixed retainer. Confirm the fit of the retainer. - Reinforce the length of retainer wear and encourage longer retainer wear time. We can slowly reduce the time to NTO when the retainer doesn't feel tight when put it back on. NV: Last retainer check in 2/16/2024 with [DOCTOR] (approx 1 year- actually 10 months), check retainer fit, build up composite on fixed retainer if needed. If everything looks good, don't need to schedule more retainer check, If patient want to come back and check then they can call the front desk to schedule.
